content.text: The shell takes it all. The meat takes the rest.
An attack is one roll: your weapon's accuracy die plus your stat bonus for that weapon (see Weapons), against the target's Evasion.

Evasion = 3 + Edge bonus + Reflex bonus, capped by armor. Heavier shells cap how high Evasion can sit — every armor row prints its cap (see Armor). Bonuses come from the one bonus table (see Stats & Growth).
Meet or beat Evasion and you hit. Land just under it and you graze — the margin and what a graze pays live on Graze & Crits. Anything lower misses.
The Shell Clock — the damage pipeline
On a hit against a shelled target, run four steps in order. Armor brings two numbers — Value (hardness) and Absorb (padding) — plus its own Armor HP. The ladder of all three lives on Armor.

Bounce gate. If your weapon's highest possible damage face is less than or equal to the armor's Value, the hit pings off — no damage, no shell wear. Tags that don't need punch-through (Direct damage) still land.
Punch-through. Subtract the armor's Value from each damage die you rolled. The sum of what's left is your punch-through.

Route it. The target's Armor HP loses the full punch-through. The target's flesh takes the punch-through minus Absorb. Absorb is padding — a router that decides how much of each hit reaches the body. It is never a second subtraction from the shell.
Break at 0 Armor HP. The shell breaks: Value and Absorb are gone, the wearer is exposed at whatever Evasion remains, and the repair bill opens (see Armor for repair and reforge).

Against a shelled target, their Armor HP is the real health bar — an armored fight is a race against the shell clock. And when your own flesh HP runs out, you drop: you are Down, and The Staircase takes over (see The Staircase).
the shell is the fight. the meat is the paperwork.

rows: Ignores Absorb: full punch-through to flesh and shell. The can caves in around them.
Full punch-through to flesh, zero shell wear. The blade finds the gap; the armor never knows.
Treat the armor's Value as X lower when resolving this attack.
Value loss that sticks: the armor's Value is permanently reduced, as printed on the effect.
Ignores everything — Value, Absorb, Armor HP. It lands even on a bounce.
+1 damage per die raises every die and your maximum face, shifting the bounce gate. This is the game's armor-piercing currency.

content.text: What you're hitting: Flesh, Hide, Shell

rows: No armor. Damage dice go straight to HP — the fastest math in the game.
Value only — no Armor HP, no Absorb, and it never breaks. You cannot grind a hide off. Penetration is the hunting tag; Pierce finds no gaps in muscle.
The full pipeline above, and it breaks. The shell clock is always running.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Pre-rebuild text lists "Predictable" as a chip tag that still applies on a bounce; the tag is not defined in the Phase 7 docs.
Armor integrity is monitored remotely for your safety. Contractors will be notified when their shell reaches zero. In most cases, they will already know.
you'll know. it's a sound you only need to hear once.
